label: markov-event-rank
headline_statement: Refuted: for \(K=\{-1,0\}\) and \(a_T=b_T=T^{-1/2}\), the block direction \(E_0+C_{11}\) has two-way residualized Gram scale at least \(T/64\), while \(\Lambda_T(K)\asymp T^{1/2}\).
witness_construction_if_refuted: Use the stationary symmetric Markov sequence a_T=b_T=T^{-1/2} with K={-1,0}. Then p_T(K)=T^{-1/2}/2 and Lambda_T(K)=(T-1)/(2 sqrt(T)), but the design span contains q=E_0+C_11, equal to D_t outside the boundary period. The two-way residualized norm of D_t is at least T/8 for large T, so the largest Gram singular value is at least T/64, violating any upper bound C_K Lambda_T(K) with C_K template-only.
